1.7: Lender does not lend where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.

5.1.1: Properties owned for less than 6 months are generally unacceptable.
Exceptions: Pound-for-pound bridging finance (including fees/interest), inherited properties with documentation, or new-builds from developers via part-exchange.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assignable contracts are prohibited.
Irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited.
5.2.1: Contact point as per 1.11a if seller is not the registered owner.
5.4.4: Environmental reports not required unless specified in the Mortgage Offer.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for re-mortgages only (excluding unencumbered properties) where the gross advance is ≤ £900,000.
Lender will arrange a Stewart Title no-search indemnity policy via Smoove (01844 262392).
Fee is deducted from the net advance.
Only Stewart Title policy via the lender's process is accepted.
Conveyancer must confirm in the Certificate of Title if this policy is required.

5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if the affected area is ≤ 25% of the total property.
5.7.1b: Exception: Single coach house flat above ≤ 4 garages where the owner uses ≥ 1 garage and the others are leased to neighbours.
Buildings insurance must cover the main walls of the garages.
5.7.1c: Report flying freeholds or accepted freehold flats to contact 1.11a.
5.8.1: Converted buildings with ≤ 4 flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold.
Conveyancer must report arrangements to 1.11a and disclose any interest the borrower has in other units.
5.8.5: Crossover leasehold/freehold arrangements for two flats accepted.
Security must include the leasehold flat and the freehold reversion of the second flat.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to contact 1.11a.
5.13.1: Report non-borrower funding or proposed second charges to contact 1.11a.

5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee/insolvent landlords accepted if building has ≤ 6 flats.
Insurance must make title good and marketable to allow unqualified COT.

5.14.1: Capital and Interest mortgages: 40 years remaining at end of term.

Historic Rent Charges: Indemnity insurance required for 110% of value, covering 121 Law of Property Act 1925 remedies.
Estate Rent Charges: Max £500 per annum, cannot double more than every 20 years.
Estate Rent Charges: Owner must contract out of Section 121 Law of Property Act 1925.
Estate Rent Charges: Requires 28 days’ notice to lender before enforcement.
Estate Rent Charges: Owner must be developer or residents' management company.
